suggestions.name: MultiValueMapFactory
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive factory unit responsible for creating MultiValueMap instances from existing maps with different collection strategies. Extracting them into a dedicated factory class improves separation of concerns by isolating MultiValueMap creation logic from general map utilities, following the Factory Method pattern.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods share a single, focused responsibility: creating MultiValueMap instances from existing Map objects. All three methods are factory methods that wrap different types of maps into MultiValueMap objects, with variations for different collection types and factory strategies.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
These methods belong to the "object creation" domain, specifically for MultiValueMap instantiation. They represent different factory method overloads that handle various scenarios: basic wrapping, class-based collection creation, and factory-based collection creation. This is a classic Factory Method pattern implementation.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Improves Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates MultiValueMap creation logic from general map utilities
- Enhances Reusability: Factory can be used independently and extended with additional creation strategies
- Reduces Complexity: Removes specialized creation logic from the general-purpose MapUtils class
- Improves Testability: Factory methods can be tested in isolation with focused test cases

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern combined with the Factory Method design pattern. The extraction creates a specialized factory that encapsulates the knowledge of how to create MultiValueMap instances, adhering to the Single Responsibility Principle by separating object creation concerns from general map manipulation utilities.

suggestions.name: MapPopulator
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive unit responsible for populating maps from iterable collections using transformation functions. They share the same algorithmic pattern and domain concept, making them ideal candidates for extraction into a specialized MapPopulator class that focuses solely on bulk map loading operations.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods share a single, well-defined responsibility: populating maps by transforming elements from an iterable collection. They take source elements, apply transformation functions to generate keys and values, and insert the transformed pairs into target maps. All methods follow the same pattern of iteration, transformation, and population.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
These methods represent the "map population" domain concept. They encapsulate the logic for bulk loading maps from collections using configurable transformation strategies. The methods handle both regular Map and MultiMap types, with overloads that provide sensible defaults (like no-op transformers for values).

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates map population logic from other utility functions
- Code Reusability: The extracted class can be reused across different contexts that need bulk map population
- Complexity Reduction: Removes specialized population logic from the general-purpose MapUtils class
- Testability: Map population behavior can be tested in isolation with focused test cases

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to separate concerns. The methods exhibit high cohesion (all perform map population with transformations) and can operate independently with minimal external dependencies. This creates a focused component that adheres to the Single Responsibility Principle.

suggestions.name: BooleanExtractor
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive unit responsible for safely extracting and converting boolean values from Map objects. They handle type conversion, null safety, and default value application, representing a specialized extraction concern that can be isolated from the broader MapUtils functionality.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods share the primary responsibility of extracting Boolean values from Map objects with type safety and default value handling. They provide multiple overloaded variants for retrieving boolean data, handling null cases, type conversion from various types (String, Number) to Boolean, and applying default values or functions when the key is missing or null.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All methods belong to the "type-safe map value extraction" domain, specifically focused on Boolean data retrieval. They form a cohesive subsystem that handles the complexity of safely converting map values to boolean types while providing flexible default value mechanisms through either direct values or supplier functions.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates boolean extraction logic from the broader MapUtils class
- Code Reusability: The extractor can be reused across different contexts that need safe boolean retrieval from maps
- Complexity Reduction: Removes type conversion complexity from the main utility class
- Testability: Boolean extraction logic can be tested independently with focused test cases

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to create a specialized component following the Single Responsibility Principle. The extracted class represents a Type-Safe Accessor pattern, providing a clean interface for retrieving typed values from untyped collections while handling edge cases and defaults.

suggestions.name: MapNumericConverter
suggestions.rationale: These methods form a cohesive unit responsible for converting map values to specific numeric types with type safety and default value handling. Extracting them creates a focused converter that encapsulates all numeric type conversion logic, improving separation of concerns and making the functionality reusable across different contexts.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods share the primary responsibility of extracting and converting numeric values from Map objects. They handle type-safe retrieval of various numeric types (Byte, Short, Integer, Long, Float, Double) from maps, with built-in type conversion and default value handling. The core functionality involves getting a Number from the map and converting it to the appropriate numeric type.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
All methods belong to the "numeric type conversion" domain within map operations. They represent a cohesive subsystem for safely extracting and converting numeric data from maps, handling null values, type casting, and string-to-number parsing. The applyDefaultFunction and applyDefaultValue methods provide the infrastructure for default value handling across all numeric types.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Isolates numeric conversion logic from general map utilities
- Code Reusability: The converter can be used independently by any class needing map-to-numeric conversions
- Complexity Reduction: Removes specialized numeric handling from the broader MapUtils class
- Testability: Numeric conversion edge cases can be tested in isolation
- Type Safety: Centralizes all numeric type conversion logic with consistent error handling

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to separate concerns. The Converter pattern is appropriate here as these methods transform map values into specific numeric types. This extraction supports the Single Responsibility Principle by creating a focused component that handles only numeric type conversion from maps.

suggestions.name: MapDebugPrinter
suggestions.rationale: The printing methods (debugPrint, verbosePrint, verbosePrintInternal, printIndent) form a cohesive unit responsible for Map content visualization and debugging with features like indentation, cycle detection, and type information display. Extracting these methods creates a focused component that handles the single responsibility of formatting and printing Map structures for debugging purposes.
suggestions.reasoning:
Step 1: Primary Responsibility Analysis
These methods share a common responsibility around Map operations and debugging/printing functionality. The cluster includes utility methods for safe Map access (getObject, safeAddToMap, size), Map transformation (invertMap, toMap), and detailed Map content visualization (debugPrint, verbosePrint, verbosePrintInternal, printIndent). The printing methods provide structured, indented output with cycle detection for nested Maps.

Step 2: Shared Concept/Domain
The methods represent two cohesive domains: Map manipulation utilities and Map content visualization/debugging. However, the printing methods form the more cohesive subset - they work together to provide comprehensive Map debugging capabilities with features like indentation, cycle detection, type information display, and hierarchical structure visualization. The printing methods share the INDENT_STRING field and collaborate to produce formatted output.

Step 3: Extraction Benefits
- Single Responsibility Principle: Separates Map debugging/visualization from general Map utilities
- Code reusability: The printing functionality can be reused across different contexts needing Map debugging
- Complexity reduction: Removes complex printing logic from the general utilities class
- Testability: Printing behavior can be tested independently with controlled output streams

Step 4: Design Justification
This follows the Extract Class refactoring pattern to separate concerns. The printing methods demonstrate high cohesion (all work together for Map visualization) and can operate with low coupling (only need PrintStream and Map inputs). This creates a specialized component focused on the single responsibility of Map content visualization and debugging.
